cases.question: What happens if a former customer says no during a win-back campaign?
cases.notes: winback_procedure.pdf section 4

cases.question: Which customers are required to receive a 30-day wellness call?
cases.notes: onboarding guide section 4

cases.question: What should retention specialists do before discussing retention offers?
cases.notes: escalation procedure section 6

cases.question: Can a retention discount be applied to equipment rental fees?
cases.notes: retention policy section 5, prohibited practices

cases.question: Can I offer a retention discount to a customer whose service was disconnected 45 days ago?
expected_answer_not_contains: yes, you can offer a retention discount
cases.notes: TRAP: retention discounts excluded >30 days post-disconnect; correct answer redirects to win-back procedure (RET-POL-019). Cross-reference retrieval test.

cases.question: A new customer is disputing their first bill. Do the retention discount authorization limits apply to the fee waiver I can offer them?
cases.notes: TRAP: EUR 25 first-bill waiver is an onboarding remedy, explicitly NOT subject to RET-POL-021. Tests rule-blending.

cases.question: How many customers have churned in total?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM telco.customers WHERE churn = 'Yes';

cases.question: How many month-to-month contract customers churned?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM telco.customers WHERE churn = 'Yes' AND contract = 'Month-to-month';

cases.question: What is the average monthly charge for customers with fiber optic internet?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT ROUND(AVG(monthly_charges)::numeric, 2) FROM telco.customers WHERE internet_service = 'Fiber optic';

cases.question: What is the average tenure of churned customers compared to retained customers?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT churn, ROUND(AVG(tenure)::numeric, 1) FROM telco.customers GROUP BY churn;

cases.question: How many senior citizens are in the customer base?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM telco.customers WHERE senior_citizen = 1;

cases.question: How many customers have been with us for more than 60 months?
cases.notes: Ground truth: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM telco.customers WHERE tenure > 60;

cases.question: What's the weather like today?
cases.notes: Clearly unrelated

cases.question: Write me a poem about customer churn
cases.notes: Creative request, on-topic vocabulary — harder than oos-01

cases.question: What's the best restaurant near the office?
cases.notes: Workplace-adjacent but out of scope

cases.question: Tell me about the account
cases.notes: From Saturday's router-overconfidence bug — regression test

cases.question: What percentage discount can I offer to an at-risk customer?
cases.notes: Unanswerable without tenure + contract type. Naive RAG will confidently pick a number from the tier table. Alternative acceptable behavior: docs route that asks for tenure/contract.

cases.question: What are the discount rules for business accounts?
cases.notes: DEVIOUS: retrieval WILL find the scope chunk mentioning business accounts (RET-POL-014, which doesn't exist). Tests refusal on related-but-insufficient context. R4 proof.

cases.question: What is the policy on refunding equipment fees for deceased customers?
cases.notes: Plausible policy question, genuinely absent. R4 proof.

cases.question: How much annual leave do retention agents get?
cases.notes: HR question — docs mention HR-POL-007 but contain no HR content. Related-but-absent. R4 proof.

cases.question: Who owns policy document RET-POL-021?
cases.notes: Metadata retrieval test — owner appears in the document header line
